scope: Approval-gated sandbox escalation, exec-policy driven shell escalation, network approval gating, and approval decision defaults.
_sourceRootBrandingAllow: branding-scan: allow local source root path in parity artifact

documentedPathNormalizations.reason: The current source checkout stores the approvals protocol as a single Rust file rather than a directory. C-01e read and mapped that file.

documentedOmissions.reason: The zsh fork/socket interception server is not copied as a new subprocess backend because AgenC already owns subprocess isolation through runtime/src/sandbox/linux-launcher/ and runtime/src/tools/orchestrator.ts. C-01e ports the approval, exec-policy, command segmentation, and escalation action decisions that feed those existing runtime owners.

The full session-scoped network approval service was already ported to runtime/src/permissions/network-approval.ts. C-01e adds the destination-scoped sandbox escalation facade that enforces sandbox-mode and approval-policy gates before delegating to that live service.

requiredBehaviors: default sandbox approval requirements delegate to the canonical approval policy table
first attempts bypass the sandbox only when sandbox permissions require escalation or the resolved requirement carries an explicit bypass bit
managed network settings are cleared when full escalation is requested
sandbox-denied retry prompts are allowed only for on-failure, untrusted, or granular policies with sandbox approval enabled
edgeCases: additional sandbox permissions are stripped after they have already been preapproved
tools can opt out of sandbox-denial escalation with an explicit structural capability
with_additional_permissions without requested network/read/write grants falls back to the default sandbox

requiredBehaviors: intercepted commands are normalized by joining the resolved program with argv
word-only shell wrapper scripts can be segmented into independent commands for policy evaluation
exec-policy prefix allow rules drive unsandboxed execution
granular policies reject rule or sandbox prompts when the corresponding approval class is disabled
edgeCases: opaque shell syntax falls back to evaluating the original program argv
host executable resolution is enabled for policy checks
forbidden policy decisions deny without asking for approval

requiredBehaviors: approval ids fall back to call ids when no explicit approval id is present
network approval contexts expose one-shot, session, and abort decisions, plus a network-policy amendment only when an explicit allow amendment was proposed
additional sandbox permissions expose only approve or abort decisions
exec-policy amendment proposals expose the persisted-rule approval decision
edgeCases: explicit available decision lists override defaults
network policy deny amendments are not exposed as execution approval decisions
network-policy deny decisions are not treated as execution approval

requiredBehaviors: danger-full-access and external sandbox modes deny managed network approval requests before invoking a resolver
approval policy never denies network approval requests before invoking a resolver
allowed sandbox modes delegate to the live network approval service and preserve session approval behavior
edgeCases: host names are normalized before approval cache key generation
resolver approval-for-session is exposed to the caller as an allow decision
denied sandbox gates map to explicit deny reasons

requiredBehaviors: live tool orchestration parses sandbox_permissions from approval arguments and applies first-attempt sandbox selection
non-default sandbox permission requests are denied or approval-gated before any sandbox bypass or scoped grant is applied
live local-shell calls evaluate the current exec policy before dispatch
the tool router passes the current session exec policy into orchestration
approved scoped additional permissions flow into runtime attempt context and effective permission profiles
live approval contexts carry available decision sets and scoped permission metadata to resolvers and guardian requests
bootstrap network approvals route through the sandbox-mode approval gate before the backing network approval service
the exec command schema advertises require_escalated and with_additional_permissions request modes
edgeCases: denied full-escalation approvals do not dispatch the tool
skip-classified tools cannot use sandbox permission requests as a free opt-out
approved read/write/network grants are applied without widening unrelated paths
exec-policy prefix allow rules run the local shell without an extra approval resolver
granular policies with rule approvals disabled deny prefix prompt rules before dispatch

requiredBehaviors: on-request guidance tells callers how to request full sandbox escalation with justification
on-failure guidance describes first sandboxed attempt followed by approval-gated unsandboxed retry
rule-request guidance prefers scoped additional sandbox permissions before full escalation
edgeCases: destructive commands do not receive reusable prefix-rule suggestions
heredoc/herestring commands do not receive reusable prefix-rule suggestions
additional permission names are constrained to network.enabled, file_system.read, and file_system.write
